scope.depth: top-level only (per Codex GO conditions; reparse points not traversed even at top)

scan_method: PowerShell Get-ChildItem + recursive size/file-count rollup (skipping reparse points). Worktree-tracking verification via git worktree list --porcelain filtered for Claude-Playground paths. Live-dependency grep across E:\GT-KB active surfaces.
credential_detection_method: Recursive filename scan: Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath E:\Claude-Playground -Force -Recurse -File -Filter '.env*'. No file content read. Path, size, last-modified captured. Per Codex GO condition 6, no secret values or content excerpts recorded.

credentials_inventory.owner_inspection_recommendation: Highest-priority files (likely real secrets vs templates/mocks) are: AGNTCY-upstream\.env, AGNTCY-upstream\.env.phase3.5, Agent Red Customer Engagement-OLD\.env.local (12 KB), and the per-app .env.local files under Agent Red Customer Engagement-OLD\admin\{provider,shopify,standalone}. Files named with .example, .mock, .production, .staging suffixes may be templates or per-environment configs. Owner should consider whether any of these archived credentials may have been exposed via Drive sync or similar before deletion authorizes — credential rotation may be appropriate independent of file deletion.
credentials_inventory.no_content_read_assertion: No file content was read during this scan. Filename, size, and last-modified timestamp only. No values, content excerpts, or hashes recorded for credential-like files.

deletion_authority_note: This manifest is read-only evidence. The owner authorizes deletion separately. No entry is presented as deletion-authorized by the manifest itself.

live_dependency_grep.raw_total_hits_in_scanned_globs: 100+ (head_limit hit; large fraction in bridge/ which is excluded)

pattern_matching_constants_in_active_scr: scripts/wrap_scan_hygiene.py:44-47 (OLD_PROJECT_ROOT_PATTERNS — strings used to FIND old-path references in other files; not a runtime path access)
scripts/migrate_root_to_gtkb.py:88-126 (one-time migration script's path-rewrite table — finds old paths to rewrite; does not depend on Claude-Playground existing)
tests/scripts/test_wrap_scan_hygiene_skip_dirs.py:20 (OLD_ROOT_TOKEN test constant — verifies wrap-scan-hygiene's old-path detection logic)
declarative_or_documentation_references: .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md (mandates Claude-Playground is not a live location)
AGENTS.md, CLAUDE-ARCHITECTURE.md (project instructions referencing the archive directive)
memory/MEMORY.md (operational state mentioning legacy paths historically)
docs/operations/*.md (historical procedure docs from pre-migration era)
independent-progress-assessments/*.md (Loyal Opposition reports referencing migration history)
scripts/archive/*.py (S117/S119/S144/S146/S153/S154/S156 handoff scripts; pre-migration era; in archive subdir)
permissions_or_settings: .claude/settings.local.json (Claude permission entries; reference patterns, not runtime paths)

live_dependency_grep.conclusion: No active GT-KB code, configuration, or runtime artifact resolves a path under E:\Claude-Playground at runtime. Every reference falls into one of three non-load-bearing categories: (1) pattern-matching constants used by hygiene/migration scripts to FIND old-path references in other files (forward-only, no Claude-Playground access), (2) declarative or documentation references (declaring or describing the archive's existence and disposition), (3) historical evidence files (handoff scripts, LO reports, approval packets, feedback memories). Removing E:\Claude-Playground would not break any runtime path resolution from inside E:\GT-KB.

summary_for_owner.deletion_readiness_status: All 16 top-level entries classified. Zero runtime path dependencies from inside E:\GT-KB to anything under E:\Claude-Playground. Zero registered git worktrees under E:\Claude-Playground (the 2 prior outside-root worktrees were pushed to origin and removed from tracking earlier this session). Zero reparse points or symlinks. 49 credential-like .env* files detected by filename only (path, size, timestamp recorded; no content read); confined to AGNTCY-upstream (5) and CLAUDE-PROJECTS (44 — split across the 'Agent Red Customer Engagement-OLD' subtree and the 2 decommissioned worktrees). Owner should consider credential exposure / rotation before deletion authorization, independently of file deletion itself. Owner-authorization-gated language used throughout; the manifest does not declare any entry deletion-ready independently of owner action.
summary_for_owner.tier_minimal_rationale: 11 entries are January-2026-or-March-2026-era ARCHIVE/EMPTY content with no live deps; deletion is candidate-after-owner-authorization with minimal review.
summary_for_owner.tier_substantive_rationale: 4 entries warrant substantive review before deletion: AGNTCY-upstream is 632 MB (per CLAUDE.md the canonical AGNTCY source is GitHub, not a local copy); CLAUDE-PROJECTS is 14.65 GB (contains the 2 decommissioned worktree directories whose branches are durable on origin, plus historical Agent Red checkouts and wiki work); groundtruth-kb is 25 MB legacy v0.3.0 superseded by v0.6.1 PyPI; .tmp.driveupload is 4.5 MB Drive sync staging that may have transient content owner wants to capture before deletion.
summary_for_owner.reserved_name_rationale: 1 entry needs owner-aware deletion: the 'nul' reserved-name file requires -LiteralPath in PowerShell or equivalent in other shells; owner authorization triggers a manifest-backed cleanup similar to the other 'nul' artifact at E:\GT-KB\nul (row 80 of sister manifest).
